Home › Orange County › Orlando › MEMORIES OF PERU II
F 58

MEMORIES OF PERU II

This grade is calculated by us from Florida DBPR public inspection records — Florida does not issue official grades. How we calculate it →

Data last updated: January 20, 2026

MEMORIES OF PERU II is a American restaurant at 5700 INTERNATIONAL DR in Orlando, Orange County, in Central Florida. At its most recent health inspection on January 20, 2026, it received a grade of F with a score of 58 out of 100. The inspection noted 2 high-priority, 5 intermediate, and 11 basic violations. By health score, it is safer than 3% of Orange County restaurants. Diners give it an average rating of 4.8 stars across 10904 reviews. Typical prices fall in the $20–30 range. Menu options are noted as suitable for vegan, vegetarian, healthy, and pescatarian diners.

Inspections trending: improving · last inspected 158 days ago

Get directions Call (407) 601-5528

Inspection history

The following records are from Florida DBPR public inspection records, most recent first.

  1. January 20, 2026 · Routine - Food

    Inspection Completed - No Further Action

    2 high-priority · 5 intermediate · 11 basic

    • 02 — Consumer advisory, date-marking & labeling of ready-to-eat foods
    • 08 — Cross-contamination: raw foods separated; food protected during prep/storage
    • 10 — In-use food dispensing utensils properly stored (×2)
    • 14 — Food-contact & nonfood-contact surfaces properly designed/maintained (×4)
    • 22 — Food-contact surfaces clean and sanitized
    • 29 — Plumbing installed & maintained; mop sink; backflow prevention
    • 31 — Handwash sinks accessible; handwashing supplies & sign provided
    • 36 — Floors, walls, ceilings clean & maintained; proper ventilation (×3)
    • 41 — Chemicals / toxic substances properly stored & used (×2)
    • 51 — Other conditions of sanitary & safe operation
    • 53 — Food manager certification; employee food-safety training

  2. July 21, 2025 · Routine - Food

    Call Back - Complied

    0 high-priority · 0 intermediate · 0 basic

    No violations cited.

  3. July 10, 2025 · Routine - Food

    Warning Issued

    3 high-priority · 7 intermediate · 19 basic

    • 02 — Consumer advisory, date-marking & labeling of ready-to-eat foods (×2)
    • 08 — Cross-contamination: raw foods separated; food protected during prep/storage (×2)
    • 10 — In-use food dispensing utensils properly stored (×2)
    • 12 — Handwashing; employee eating/drinking/smoking practices (×2)
    • 14 — Food-contact & nonfood-contact surfaces properly designed/maintained (×4)
    • 22 — Food-contact surfaces clean and sanitized (×2)
    • 24 — Storage & handling of clean equipment and utensils; air drying
    • 25 — Single-service and single-use items
    • 29 — Plumbing installed & maintained; mop sink; backflow prevention
    • 31 — Handwash sinks accessible; handwashing supplies & sign provided (×4)
    • 33 — Garbage and refuse; premises maintained
    • 36 — Floors, walls, ceilings clean & maintained; proper ventilation (×4)
    • 41 — Chemicals / toxic substances properly stored & used (×2)
    • 51 — Other conditions of sanitary & safe operation
